mechanism_narrative: Persistent elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL above trend) erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank toward tightening, which compresses equity valuations. Over a 20-day horizon, high inflation readings consistently predict negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months with CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, or if the rank IC of CPIAUCSL level on forward returns is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Very high unemployment rates (UNRATE > 8%) signal deep economic slack, prompting expectations of aggressive monetary or fiscal easing. This anticipated policy support drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as markets price in recovery.
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are negative in at least 40% of triggered periods (UNRATE>8%) over any 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate robust consumer demand without monetary headwinds, a classic 'Goldilocks' environment that boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations. The joint signal is stronger than either alone.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (RSAFS growth * low FEDFUNDS dummy) has no significant positive coefficient in regression of forward 20-day returns, or if Spearman rank correlation of the interaction signal with returns is below 0.05 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime (specifically when unemployment is elevated but falling), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals the start of a durable expansion phase, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days as cyclical earnings improve.
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns during recovery regimes with rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.1) or if the INDPRO change signal has negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained robust payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate profits and equity prices higher. The signal is strongest when changes exceed 500,000 over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in more than half of trigger events, or if the strategy's information ratio is below 0.2 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator for residential investment and consumer confidence. Increases in housing starts presage broader economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over short horizons (1 week).
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following positive monthly housing starts changes is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or if the correlation is negative over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is moderate, the economy operates at an ideal mix of growth and price stability, supporting equity prices. The interaction between GDP growth and low inflation provides a more reliable positive signal for 20-day returns than either alone.
failure_condition: If the combined signal (GDP growth minus inflation adjusted) fails to have a positive rank IC over 20-day forward returns in two consecutive four-quarter windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, defined by FEDFUNDS rising above 1% and a 3-month trend increase of at least 25 basis points, higher interest rates increase discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of tightening episodes, or if the mean return during trigger periods is not significantly less than zero (t-test p>0.1).
